question: Dogecoin Up or Down - March 21, 2:00PM-2:15PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Dogecoin price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the DOGE/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/doge-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream DOGE/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Warriors vs. Pistons: 1H O/U 104.5
description:
In the first half of the NBA game between Warriors and Pistons, scheduled for March 20 at 7:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Warriors and Pistons combine to score 105 or more points in the first half.

If the combined first half total is less than 105, this market will resolve to "Under".

The result will be determined based on the score at halftime only.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Warriors vs. Pistons
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for March 20 at 7:30PM ET:
If the Warriors win, the market will resolve to "Warriors".
If the Pistons win, the market will resolve to "Pistons".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Detroit Pistons' 75.5% implied probability reflects trader consensus on Golden State Warriors' depleted roster amid a brutal injury wave, with Stephen Curry sidelined by a deep thigh bruise, Jonathan Kuminga out with thigh soreness, and Buddy Hield questionable with an ankle tweak per official reports. Pistons ride a hot streak, winners of four straight behind Cade Cunningham's explosive 29.5 points per game average and top-10 defense clamping opponents to 98 points nightly. Warriors limp in at 1-4 road form, outscored by 12 per 100 possessions without Curry, while Detroit's athletic frontcourt exploits Golden State's thin wing depth in this key Eastern Conference tilt. Upsets remain possible given NBA volatility.

question: Will Anthropic have the best AI model at the end of April 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena score based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Score" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ranked primarily by their arena score at this market’s check time, with alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group used as a tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus pins Anthropic at a 63% implied probability for the top AI model on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena by April's end, anchored by Claude 3 Opus's persistent Elo lead from sustained user votes. Mistral (40.5%), Zhipu AI (38.0% as Z.ai), and Meituan (36.5%) surge on recent launches: Mistral Large's coding benchmarks, Zhipu GLM-4's multimodal gains, and Meituan Doubao-Pro 1.5's April 23 release topping Chinese evals. OpenAI (28%) and Google (26%) trail amid vote volatility, with ByteDance, Moonshot, and DeepSeek riding domestic benchmark hype but facing global scrutiny—traders eye final Arena updates before May releases disrupt standings.

question: Will DeepSeek have the second best AI model at the end of April 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the second-highest arena score based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Score" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ranked primarily by their arena score at this market’s check time, with alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group used as a tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

question: Spread: Stars (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 21 at 4:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Stars" if the Stars win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Wild".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Stars vs. Wild
events.description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 21 at 4:00PM ET:
If the Stars win, the market will resolve to "Stars".
If the Wild win, the market will resolve to "Wild".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Dallas Stars hold a slight edge in trader consensus for their Central Division clash with the Minnesota Wild, driven by superior recent form with four wins in their last five games compared to the Wild's 2-3 skid amid goaltending instability. Official injury reports confirm Stars forward Mason Marchment is day-to-day with a lower-body issue but practiced fully, while Wild captain Jared Spurgeon remains sidelined long-term by a hip injury, weakening their blue line. Home-ice advantage at Xcel Energy Center favors Minnesota historically (6-4 last 10 meetings), but Dallas's rest edge after a lighter schedule and potent power play (22.5% efficiency) tilt probabilities. Momentum and depth position the Stars as consensus favorites, though Wild's desperation for playoff positioning adds upset potential.

This market will resolve to "Up" if the Bitcoin price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Will Anthropic have the third best AI model at the end of April 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the third-highest arena score based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Score" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ranked primarily by their arena score at this market’s check time, with alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group used as a tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies third place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions Google as the frontrunner at 65.5% implied probability for the third-best AI model by end of April, anchored by Gemini 1.5 Pro's strong LMSYS Chatbot Arena Elo score of around 1270, trailing only OpenAI's GPT-4 Turbo and Anthropic's Claude 3 Opus/Sonnet in verified leaderboards. xAI surged to 38% following Grok-1.5's April 12 release, showcasing competitive coding and math benchmarks, while Meituan's 37% reflects buzz around its proprietary LLM optimizations for e-commerce tasks amid Chinese model advances. OpenAI (36.5%) and Anthropic (36%) hover due to leaderboard volatility; traders eye final Arena updates and MMLU evals before resolution, with timelines prone to slips.

This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the third-highest arena score based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Score" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ranked primarily by their arena score at this market’s check time, with alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group used as a tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies third place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions Google as the frontrunner at 65.5% implied probability for the third-best AI model by end of April, anchored by Gemini 1.5 Pro's strong LMSYS Chatbot Arena Elo score of around 1270, trailing only OpenAI's GPT-4 Turbo and Anthropic's Claude 3 Opus/Sonnet in verified leaderboards. xAI surged to 38% following Grok-1.5's April 12 release, showcasing competitive coding and math benchmarks, while Meituan's 37% reflects buzz around its proprietary LLM optimizations for e-commerce tasks amid Chinese model advances. OpenAI (36.5%) and Anthropic (36%) hover due to leaderboard volatility; traders eye final Arena updates and MMLU evals before resolution, with timelines prone to slips.

question: Solana Up or Down - March 21, 2:25PM-2:30PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Solana price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the SOL/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/sol-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream SOL/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Will Anthropic have the #1 AI model at the end of April 2026 (Style Control On)?
description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena score (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Score" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ranked primarily by their arena score at this market’s check time, with alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group used as a tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic's Claude 3 Opus commands a 56% implied probability as the top AI model by April's end, anchored by its leading 1285 Elo score on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard, where it has held firm amid minor fluctuations. OpenAI's GPT-4 Turbo trails closely at 1280 Elo with 42.5% odds, reflecting trader bets on potential late-month tweaks overtaking the leader. Chinese contenders like ByteDance's Doubao, Baidu's Ernie, DeepSeek, and Alibaba's Qwen cluster around 35-36% due to their top-10 rankings and rapid benchmark gains from April releases, though no disruptive launches materialized by month's close to unseat Anthropic. Google, xAI, and others lag slightly, with market consensus pricing leaderboard stability over speculation.

This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena score (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Score" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ranked primarily by their arena score at this market’s check time, with alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group used as a tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic's Claude 3 Opus commands a 56% implied probability as the top AI model by April's end, anchored by its leading 1285 Elo score on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard, where it has held firm amid minor fluctuations. OpenAI's GPT-4 Turbo trails closely at 1280 Elo with 42.5% odds, reflecting trader bets on potential late-month tweaks overtaking the leader. Chinese contenders like ByteDance's Doubao, Baidu's Ernie, DeepSeek, and Alibaba's Qwen cluster around 35-36% due to their top-10 rankings and rapid benchmark gains from April releases, though no disruptive launches materialized by month's close to unseat Anthropic. Google, xAI, and others lag slightly, with market consensus pricing leaderboard stability over speculation.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - March 21, 2:35PM-2:40PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Bitcoin price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Anthropic (58%) to claim the #2 spot on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena "Style Control On" leaderboard by April 30, propelled by Claude 3 Opus's persistent high Elo ratings in style-tuned evals, distinguishing it from leader GPT-4 Turbo. Google trails at 39.5% amid Gemini 1.5 Pro's multimodal gains but faces slippage risks from leaderboard volatility. Chinese contenders like Moonshot (38%), Meituan (36.5%), and Mistral (36.5%) surge on recent releases—Kimi-long's context window expansion and Doubao's compute-fueled benchmarks—challenging Western dominance. Zhipu, Baidu, and xAI round out leaders via Ernie 4.0 previews and Grok optimizations, with traders eyeing final-week battles before snapshot. OpenAI lags as its depth models underperform in style control.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - March 21, 2:40PM-2:45PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Bitcoin price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Will Anthropic have the #3 AI model at the end of April 2026 (Style Control On)?
description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the third-highest arena score (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Score" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ranked primarily by their arena score at this market’s check time, with alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group used as a tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies third place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the #3 AI model spot by April's end in LMSYS Chatbot Arena's Style Control mode hinges on a dead heat between Google (44%) and xAI (44%), driven by recent leaderboard volatility from Gemini 1.5 Pro's strong Elo gains and Grok-1.5V's multimodal benchmarks. OpenAI's GPT-4o and Anthropic's Claude 3.5 Sonnet hold top-two dominance, leaving the bronze medal contested amid Chinese challengers like Alibaba's Qwen 1.5 and DeepSeek-V2 surging on open-source efficiency. Differentiators include Google's massive context windows versus xAI's real-time data integration, but final rankings depend on late-April battle votes; historical slips favor established labs over rapid releases.

description:
In the upcoming match between Power Rangers and AVULUS in the PREMIER SERIES Play-In Group A, initially scheduled for March 20 at 1:30PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 47 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

events.title: Dota 2: Power Rangers vs AVULUS (BO3) - PREMIER SERIES Play-In Group A
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Power Rangers and AVULUS in the PREMIER SERIES Play-In Group A, initially scheduled for March 20 at 1:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Power Rangers" if Power Rangers win the match against AVULUS.

This market will resolve to "AVULUS" if AVULUS win the match against Power Rangers.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Power Rangers hold a slight edge in trader consensus for their BO3 matchup against AVULUS in PREMIER SERIES Play-In Group A, driven by stronger recent form with two straight series wins, including a 2-0 over a mid-tier opponent last week. AVULUS struggles with inconsistency, dropping their last three maps amid reported draft struggles on the current patch. Head-to-head favors Power Rangers 3-1 over five meetings, though AVULUS boasts a strong home map pool on Ancient. No roster changes confirmed; watch for first-game draft momentum and player execution in high-pressure play-ins, where upsets occur in 25% of similar BO3s. Schedule rest gives neither an advantage.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - March 22, 3PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: Spread: CD Leganés (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming La Liga 2 game, scheduled for April 2 at 3:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "CD Leganés" if CD Leganés win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Real Zaragoza".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: CD Leganés vs. Real Zaragoza - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga 2 game, scheduled for April 2 at 3:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: CD Leganés holds a clear edge in trader consensus for their home clash with Real Zaragoza in LaLiga 2, with implied probabilities reflecting Leganés' solid defensive form (conceding just 0.8 goals per game at Butarque) and Zaragoza's dismal away record (winless in six road matches). Recent developments include Leganés confirming full fitness for key midfielder Javi Hernández after a minor knock, while Zaragoza suspended forward Iván Azón due to accumulation of cards, weakening their attack. Leganés rides a three-game unbeaten streak, bolstered by home crowd energy, whereas Zaragoza struggles post-international break with fatigue from a congested schedule. Upcoming factors like Leganés' promotion push and Zaragoza's injury doubts further tilt sentiment toward the hosts.

question: Solana Up or Down - March 22, 3PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the SOL/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.
